title: How did the Messiah weave together Torah and Prophets when he said super happy are the pure in heart for they shall see God
author: joshualinog
excerpt: # How did the Messiah weave together Torah and Prophets when he said super happy are the pure in heart for they shall see God
body:
# How did the Messiah weave together Torah and Prophets when he said super happy are the pure in heart for they shall see God 

## Matthew 5:8
BIB Μακάριοι Makarioi Blessed [are] οἱ hoi the καθαροὶ katharoi pure τῇ tē - καρδίᾳ kardia in heart , Ὅτι Hoti for αὐτοὶ autoi they τὸν ton - Θεὸν Theon God . ὄψονται opsontai will see
YLT 'Happy the clean in heart--because they shall see God.
LEB Blessed are the pure in heart, because they will see God.
OHBa
HB2 5:8 אַשְׁרֵי בָּרֵי לֵבָב, כִּי הֵם יִרְאוּ אֶת אֱלֹהִים.
BHSE
ABP μακάριοι Blessed are οι the καθαροί clean τη καρδία in heart, ότι for αυτοί they τον θεόν όψονται shall see God.
ALXX
SBLGNT μακάριοι οἱ καθαροὶ τῇ καρδίᾳ, ὅτι αὐτοὶ τὸν θεὸν ὄψονται.


## passages 
Psalms 51:10
LEB Create a clean heart for me, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
HB2 51:12 לֵב טָהוֹר בְּרָא־לִי אֱלֹהִים וְרוּחַ נָכוֹן חַדֵּשׁ בְּקִרְבִּי:

Habakkuk 1:13
LEB Your eyes are too pure to see evil, and you are not able to look at wrongdoing. Why do you look at the treacherous? Why are you silent when the wicked swallows up someone more righteous than him?
SBLGNT
HB2 1:13 טְהוֹר עֵינַיִם מֵרְאוֹת רָע וְהַבִּיט אֶל־עָמָל לֹא תוּכָל לָמָּה תַבִּיט בּוֹגְדִים תַּחֲרִישׁ בְּבַלַּע רָשָׁע צַדִּיק מִמֶּנּוּ:

## καθαρός lexical notes
- καθαρός, -ά, -όν
[in LXX chiefly for טָהוֹר ;]

- G2513 καθαρός (katharós｜kath-ar-os'｜adjective｜clean)------------
[Grk] καθαρός LN: 53.29, 79.48 GK: G2754 Hebrew: בַּר‎, זַךְ‎, זָכָה‎, זָרָה‎, טָהֹור‎, טָהֵר‎, יֹשֶׁר‎, כּוּן‎, מִכְלָאָה‎, מָרָק‎, נְקֵא‎, נָקָה‎, נָקִי‎, סָגַר‎, קָדֹושׁ‎, שַׁחַת‎, תָּו‎, תֹּם‎------------
Derivation: of uncertain affinity;
Strong's: clean (literally or figuratively)
KJV: --clean, clear, pure.

- G2513 * καθαρός (katharos)

katharos H1249 * בַּר (bar) bar

katharos H2134 * זַךְ (zaḵ) zakh

katharos H2135 * זָכַה (zāḵah) zakhah hithp.

katharos H2219 * זָרַה (zārah) zarah

katharos H2889 * טָהֹר טָהוֹר (ṭāhōr ṭāhôr) #tahor

katharos H2891 * טָהֵר (ṭāhēr) taher qal,pi,hithp

katharos H3476 * יֹשֶׁר (yōšer) yosher

katharos H3559 * כּוּן (kûn) kun hi.

katharos H4356 * מִכְלָה מִכְלָה (miḵəlāh miḵəlāh) mikhlah

katharos H4839 * מָרַק (māraq) maraq

katharos H5343 * נְקֵא (nəqē') neqe

katharos H5352 * נָקַה (nāqah) naqah ni.

katharos H5355 * נָקִיא נָקִי (nāqî' nāqî) naqi

katharos H5462 * סָגַר (sāḡar) sagar

katharos H6918 * קָדֹשׁ קָדוֹשׁ (qāḏōš qāḏôš) qadosh

katharos H7819 * שָׁחַט (šāḥaṭ) shachat

katharos H2869 * טָב (ṭāḇ) tav

katharos H8537 * תֹּם (tōm) tom


G2513

Original: καθαρός

Transliteration: katharos

Phonetic: kath-ar-os'

Thayer Definition:

1. clean, pure
   1. physically
      1. purified by fire
      2. in a similitude, like a vine cleansed by pruning and so fitted to bear fruit
   2. in a levitical sense
      1. clean, the use of which is not forbidden, imparts no uncleanness
   3. ethically
      1. free from corrupt desire, from sin and guilt
      2. free from every admixture of what is false, sincere genuine
      3. blameless, innocent
      4. unstained with the guilt of anything

Origin: of uncertain affinity

TDNT entry: 09:53,4

Part(s) of speech: Adjective

Strong's Definition: Of uncertain affinity; clean (literally or figuratively): - clean, clear, pure.

Total KJV Occurrences: 27

are the pure (1)
Mat 5:8

clean (8)
Mat 23:26; Luk 11:41; Joh 13:10; Joh 13:10; Joh 13:11; Joh 15:3; Rev 19:8; Rev 19:14 (refs7)

in a clean (1)
Mat 27:59

am clean (1)
Act 18:6

am pure (1)
Act 20:26

are pure (1)
Rom 14:20

a pure (5)
1Ti 1:5; 1Ti 3:9; 2Ti 2:22; 1Pe 1:22; Rev 22:1 (refs5)

pure (3)
2Ti 1:3; Tit 1:15; Jas 1:27 (refs3)

unto the pure (1)
Tit 1:15

with pure (1)
Heb 10:22

in pure (1)
Rev 15:6

was pure (2)
Rev 21:18; Rev 21:21 (refs2)

unto clear (1)
Rev 21:18
